full_response:
We compute the partial derivatives of \(f_{A,b}(\vec{x}) = \frac{1}{2} \vec{x}^\top (A^\top A) \vec{x} - \vec{b}^\top A \vec{x}\) for \(A = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ -1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}\) and \(\vec{b} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 3 \end{bmatrix}\).

First, find \(A^\top A\):
\[
A^\top = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & -1 \\ 2 & 2 \end{bmatrix}, \quad A^\top A = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & -1 \\ 2 & 2 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ -1 & 2 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 0 \\ 0 & 8 \end{bmatrix}.
\]

Then,
\[
\frac{1}{2} \vec{x}^\top (A^\top A) \vec{x} = \frac{1}{2} \begin{bmatrix} x_1 & x_2 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 0 \\ 0 & 8 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} x_1 \\ x_2 \end{bmatrix} = \frac{1}{2} (2x_1^2 + 8x_2^2) = x_1^2 + 4x_2^2.
\]

Next, compute \(A\vec{x}\) and \(\vec{b}^\top A \vec{x}\):
\[
A\vec{x} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ -1 & 2 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} x_1 \\ x_2 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} x_1 + 2x_2 \\ -x_1 + 2x_2 \end{bmatrix}, \quad \vec{b}^\top A \vec{x} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 3 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} x_1 + 2x_2 \\ -x_1 + 2x_2 \end{bmatrix} = (x_1 + 2x_2) + 3(-x_1 + 2x_2) = -2x_1 + 8x_2.
\]

Thus,
\[
f_{A,b}(\vec{x}) = x_1^2 + 4x_2^2 - (-2x_1 + 8x_2) = x_1^2 + 4x_2^2 + 2x_1 - 8x_2.
\]

Partial derivatives:
\[
\frac{\partial f}{\partial x_1} = 2x_1 + 2, \quad \frac{\partial f}{\partial x_2} = 8x_2 - 8.
\]

This matches option C.

\boxed{C}
Confidence: 100%
